digest.component_or_area: code-review block gate (code-v2 impact model threshold calibration)
propositions: The code-review gate cast zero BLOCK votes across 162 changes despite advisories and confirmed must-fix findings, because the 0.95 default threshold is unreachable given the observed impact ceiling near 0.60.

The correct block threshold must be derived empirically as the precision-crossover point where a finding is more likely correctly-blocking than false-blocking, selecting the smallest candidate t where precision(block-worthy | priority >= t) exceeds 0.5.
Precision must be computed over PRIORITY (validity × impact), the exact quantity the gate thresholds on, not raw impact.

A dedicated derivation script must load code_review_result_v1 sidecars (blocking/advisory buckets) reading stored priority directly, since the plan-review calibrator cannot consume the code-review schema.

The impact-model separation gate (ab_impact_model.py over the static HIGH/NIT fixture) is a distinct model-quality prerequisite and must not be conflated with deriving the threshold from the real corpus.

An adjudicated corpus and a threshold recommendation must be committed as verifiable repo artifacts under docs/experiments/, with derivation logic unit-tested under tests/, including 0.54-band and 0.60-ceiling analyses.
